title: Regression on RangeSlider clearing min max boundaries
body:
### 🐛 Current behavior

The new library `instantsearch.js` differs from the old library `react-instantsearch-dom` when clearing refinements with `ClearRefinements` components on `RangeSlider` components with `min` or `max` properties.

For the old behavior, you can see it here : [storybook](https://instantsearchjs.netlify.app/stories/react/?path=/story/rangeslider--custom-min-max-bounds)

On start, using `RangeSlider(min = 30, max = 100)` a query is made with `numericFilters: ["price>=30","price<=100"]` and when updating the slider, new queries are made like `numericFilters: ["price>=42","price<=50"]`. When clicking `Clear all filters`, the slider is reset to `(min = 30, max = 100)` and a query of `numericFilters: ["price>=30","price<=100"]` is made (hitting the cache).

This is not the case anymore in `instantsearch.js`

### 🔍 Steps to reproduce

On the provided sandbox, with network devtools open:

On start, we have `rangeSlider({ min: 10, max: 500 })`

1. A query is made with `numericFilters: ["price>=10","price<=500"]`
2. After updating the slide, new queries are made like `numericFilters: ["price>=40","price<=100"]`
3. Clicking `Clear refinements`
4. Ui of Slider is reset to `10 -> 500`
5. An empty query (without `numericFilters`) is made

### Live reproduction

https://codesandbox.io/embed/mystifying-joliot-eb049f?fontsize=14&hidenavigation=1&theme=dark

### 💭 Expected behavior

On the last step (after clicking `Clear refinements`) a query with the min max boundaries `numericFilters: ["price>=10","price<=500"]` as defined by the component should be made instead of an empty one (thus ignoring components properties). 

### Package version

instantsearch.js 4.51.1

### Operating system

macOS 13.0.1

### Browser

Chrome 110

### Code of Conduct

- [X] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct
